prerequisites: A Harness account. Find your account ID (account_id / accountIdentifier) in any Harness platform URL, for example https://app.harness.io/ng/#/account/ACCOUNT_ID/home/get-started.
A Harness API key token created from your user profile under My API Keys. The token is shown only once at creation, so store it securely (api_key).
For service-account API keys, the Account Admin role or equivalent permission to view, create/edit, manage, and delete service accounts. Personal API keys require no special role.
Optionally the API base URL (api_url) if you use a self-hosted or vanity endpoint instead of the default https://app.harness.io.

authMethods.name: API key (x-api-key token)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Harness API key token. Create an API key in your user profile under My API Keys, generate a token under it, and supply that token as the api_key secret. Harness sends it in the x-api-key request header, and calls also require your account_id (accountIdentifier). Tokens inherit the permissions of the associated user or service account.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key and token in Harness
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Harness and open My Profile. Under My API Keys, select +API Key and give it a name. Within the new API key, select +Token, enter a token name, optionally set an expiration (30, 90, 180 days, or a custom date), and choose Generate Token. Copy and store the token immediately, because Harness displays it only once and does not store it for later retrieval.

setupSteps.title: Find your account ID
setupSteps.body: Copy your account identifier from any Harness platform URL. For example, in https://app.harness.io/ng/#/account/ACCOUNT_ID/home/get-started the ACCOUNT_ID segment is the value to use for the account_id field (sent as the accountIdentifier query parameter on API calls).

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set account_id to your Harness account identifier and api_key to the generated API token. Leave api_url at its default (https://app.harness.io) unless you connect to a self-hosted or vanity Harness endpoint, in which case set api_url to that base URL.
